title: REPLACE PARTITION silently fails when one of the partition keys is of type Bool
description:
### Company or project name
CloudQuery
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
When using `REPLACE PARTITION` on two tables that have a Bool-type column as part of the partition key, the operation succeeds but actually does not do a replacement. The target table's data stays the same as before.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
Tested with ClickHouse Server 25.6.13 and 25.10.2, both show this behavior.
### How to reproduce
Using 25.10.2.
### 1. Create tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE target_table
(
id UInt32,
country String,
active Bool
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
PARTITION BY (country, active)
ORDER BY id;
CREATE TABLE source_table
(
id UInt32,
country String,
active Bool
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
PARTITION BY (country, active)
ORDER BY id;
```
### 2. Insert initial data
Old data in `target_table`:
```sql
INSERT INTO target_table VALUES
(1, 'US', 1),
(2, 'US', 1),
(3, 'UK', 0);
```
New data in `source_table`:
```sql
INSERT INTO source_table VALUES
(10, 'US', 1),
(20, 'US', 1);
```
### 3a. Verify initial target contents
```
SELECT *, _partition_id
FROM target_table
ORDER BY id, country, active;
```
Should see:
poc:
CREATE TABLE target_table
(
    id UInt32,
    country String,
    active Bool
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
PARTITION BY (country, active)
ORDER BY id;

CREATE TABLE source_table
(
    id UInt32,
    country String,
    active Bool
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
PARTITION BY (country, active)
ORDER BY id;
key_bug_features: REPLACE PARTITION with Bool-type column fails silently
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE target_table_2 (id UInt32, country String, active Bool, created_at DateTime) ENGINE = MergeTree() PARTITION BY (country, active) ORDER BY id;
CREATE TABLE source_table_2 (id UInt32, country String, active Bool, created_at DateTime) ENGINE = MergeTree() PARTITION BY (country, active) ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO target_table_2 VALUES (1, 'US', 1, now()), (2, 'US', 1, now()), (3, 'UK', 0, now());
INSERT INTO source_table_2 VALUES (10, 'US', 1, now()), (20, 'US', 1, now()), (30, 'UK', 0, now());
ALTER TABLE target_table_2 REPLACE PARTITION ('US', 1) FROM source_table_2;
